titleStmt.title: National Library of Sweden, A 56
titleStmt.sponsor: Medieval Latin Manuscripts in the National Library: Cataloguing and Digitization
titleStmt.funder: Riksbankens jubileumsfond – The Swedish Foundation for Humanities and Social Sciences

publicationStmt.publisher: manuscripta.se: A Digital Catalogue of Medieval and Early Modern Manuscripts in Sweden

supportDesc.foliation: Modern foliation in pencil. REgnante illustrissimo rege

p.note: Top left corner: National Library book label.
Paper note glued to page, in ink by a modern hand: list of saints.
Top of page, in ink by a modern hand: sign. A. 56 i Antiqvitets Arkivet..

binding.p: Modern binding. Leather and marbled paper over cardboard.

origin.p:
Finland Turku , 15th century, last quarter, 16th century, first quarter. Written and owned by Nils Jakobsson Byrkop. His name is found on : Nicolaus est pres huius libri; : Nicolaus Iacobi Byrkop est pres huius Codicis; : Qui me scribebat nicolaus nomen habebat. For more on Nils, see Maliniemi (1957)
.

provenance.p: In the 18th century the manuscript belonged to the Antiquities Archive. There, it was listed in the invetories as: no. 151, Lectiones historicae de sanctis Sveciae et patronis. Ms papper, (U 90:1, f. 13r);

no. 24 Lectiones historicae de sanctis Regni Sueciae Martyribus et patronis olim in eclesiis Suecanis usitate, (U 90:2); the same title is found on ; no. 24, (U 90:4, p. 79); Andelige böcker på papper skrifne in Quarto O 24, (U 91:1); O 24 (U 91:2:1); A 56, (U 93).

acquisition.p: From the Antiquities Archive to the Royal library in .

msItem.title: Office for St Henrik (20 Jan.)
msItem.finalRubric: Explicit hic cantus henrici dummodo tantus

msItem.title: Office for St Erik (18 May)
msItem.rubric: Historia De Sancto Erico martire et rege
msItem.incipit: Assunt erici regis solennia laude regem
msItem.explicit: nobis placa martir bone ihesum dei filium alleluia
msItem.note: , bottom margin: a distich in hexameter in red: Presul et Henricus servant nos sanctus Ericus / Sic fugit iniqus hos diligit omnis amicus.

msItem.title: Office for St Erasmus (3 June)
msItem.incipit: Oracio Da nobis quesumus omnipotens deus Vt quyi beati erasmi
msItem.explicit: a sancti angelis cum gloria in secula seculorum Amen Tu

msItem.title: Office for St Eskil (12 June)
msItem.incipit: Oracio antiphona supra psalmum Letabitur Responsorium O proles britanie
msItem.explicit: tue sis familie dulcis Consolator Amen

msItem.title: Office for St Sunniva (8 July)
msItem.rubric: In festo Sancte suniue virginis et martiris sociorum eius
msItem.explicit: cui est honor et gloria in secula Seculorum Amen

msItem.title: Office for St Elin of Skövde (31 July)
msItem.incipit: Capitulum militem fortem FElix parens vesgocia
msItem.explicit: vivamus eterna Magnificat anima Mea dominum

msItem.title: Office for St Barbara (4 Dec.)
msItem.incipit: Capitulum Confitebeor Optaui et datus est michi sensus
msItem.explicit: atque saluatore nostro ihesu christo in secula seculorum Amen

msItem.title: Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary (21 Nov.)
msItem.rubric: In festo presentacionis Sanctissime virgine marie
msItem.incipit: Expedit fidelium deuocioni beatissime virginis marie
msItem.explicit: et post hanc fragilem vitam felicitatem eternam nobis prestare dignetur Tu autem antiphona Deo gracias

msItem.title: Office for St Knut (7 Jan.)
msItem.rubric: kanuto Die Sancto kanuto rege et martire
msItem.incipit: oracio Deus qui Beatum kanutum regem
msItem.explicit: de quibus longum est per singula enarrare Tu autem

msItem.title: Office for St Ansgar (4 Feb.)
msItem.rubric: In festo Sancti ansgarii episcopi et confessoris
msItem.incipit: Deus qui populo tuo eterne salutis beatum ansgarium
msItem.explicit: per tres menses continuo infirmatus Die Beati blasii quieuit in pace Tu autem Domine

msItem.title: Office for St Dorothea (6 Feb.)
msItem.incipit: Oracio Omnipotens sempiterne deus in cuius nomine gloriosa virgo et martir dorothea
msItem.explicit: Sic quod theophilus ad christum perrexit Tu

msItem.title: Office for St Simeon (9 Feb.)
msItem.incipit: Collecta collecta PErfice in nobis quesumus Domine graciam tuam qui iusti Symeonis exspectacionem implesti
msItem.finalRubric: Cetera omnia Sicut in communi vnius confessoris

msItem.title: Office for St Sigfrid (15 Feb.)
msItem.explicit: sine terminacione tecum celi mansione Magnificat anima mea dominum

msItem.title: Office for St Gertrude (17 Mar.)
msItem.incipit: Oracio Deus qui Beatam Gertrudem Virginem
msItem.explicit: Cuius animama sancti angeli ad celestia gaudia detulerant Tu autem Deo Gracias
msItem.note: Below the main text: A baratri pena salua nos virgo serena.

msItem.title: Compassio Mariae
msItem.rubric: Historia De Compassione Beate marie virginis
msItem.incipit: STabat virgo dolorosa mater christi generosa
msItem.explicit: nobis donet sine fine perfrui leticia alleluia Magnificat anima

msItem.title: Office for the Patron Saints of Sweden (3-9 July)
msItem.incipit: IOcundare mater ecclesia noue lucis agens solennia
msItem.explicit: In qua semper gloriantur beatorum anime psalmus Magnificat anima

msItem.title: Office for St Birgitta (23 July)
msItem.explicit: Signum magnum apparvit amicta sole femina solem

msItem.title: Office for St Christoffer (25 July)
msItem.incipit: Oracio Omnipotens sempiterne Deus fortitudo constantium
msItem.explicit: ut si quis deum aut christoforum blasphemaret continuo gladio feriretur Tu autem

watermark.term: The letter P; above, single line rod ending in flower with four petals.

supportDesc.foliation: Contemporary foliation in Roman numerals in the top margin.
supportDesc.condition: Leaves in poor condition, water damage, tears, and stains throughout, e.g. , , . Leaves missing at the end.

handDesc.handNote: Gothic semihybrida. Scribe: Nils Jakobsson Byrkop.

p.note:
The enitre page is covered in pen-trials by the scribe. For more on the additions here and on 53v, see Maliniemi (1957)
.

Upper part of page, in bronw ink by a medieval hand: a fragmented prayer; middle of page, in ink by an early modern hand: Lectiones historia de sanctis Regni Sueciae Martyribus et patronis olim in Ecclesiis Suecanis usitate.
Additions in the margins by one or serveral contemporary hands, e.g. on or .
Upper right corner, in ink by a modern hand: Scr. Rer. Suec. II. (text indentification)

Four different texts by the same hand, the first two are written in a faded ink. Text 1 carrries the title Contra pulices; text 2 is 10 line long poem beginning: O doctor moris; text 3 is in Swedish and seems to be a recipe against ringworm (raefform); text 4 has the title: De decimis et oblacionibus.

A text in Swedish, but the leaf is damaged and the ink faded. It seems to be related to Läke- och örteböcker från Sveriges medeltid
.
A few words or parts of words can be seen on the stubs. They seem to be written by the same hand as the other additions.

decoDesc.p: Main text in brown ink, rubrics in red (green on ), capitals touched in red.

decoNote.p: : Opening plain initial G over 3 lines in red.
: plain initials over 2 lines in red. On the initals are written in green. : flourish in red added to the last initial on the page.
: initial outlined in black in, filled with red and black.
: cadel intial I over 4-6 lines in black, filled in with red.
